sentence: Never mangoe a brousin who carried you home last winter.

wombos.definition: A bro who feels like family — someone with the closeness of a cousin but the bond of a best friend. Used as a term of address or reference: 'that's my brousin,' 'brousin, you already know.'
To potentially square up with someone — the conditional, fruit-coded form of 'may go toe to toe.' Implies a fight could break out but hasn't yet.
wombos.example: Keep talking and we mangoe right here.
